title: Let's Objectify Some Plastic - featuring Zombicide Invader
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=V3IHs2DwX0U

In this "Objectifying Plastic" episode, Daniel (@dungeondive) builds his six-hero party for **Zombicide: Invader** — the sci-fi entry — by walking the entire miniatures collection and picking largely on the strength of their designs and the pop-culture properties they parody.

Up front he airs his biggest gripe with all of Zombicide: the mandatory six-character party. It's a lot of powers to track and a lot of table space, and he'd happily play with four (or fewer) if the game allowed — a request he throws out to viewers for house rules. Invader splits its roster into civilians (search freely, less armour) and soldiers (more armour, limited search), and you're meant to field three of each.

The rest is an affectionate tour of the sculpts, most of them nods to films Daniel loves: Vasquez from *Aliens*, Judge Dredd, Emily Blunt's soldier from *Edge of Tomorrow*, Tank Girl, a Stephen-Hawking-inspired genius, and a District 9-style insectoid he picks purely for the mini. His verdict on the starting six that come in the box: the most boring of the lot. This is the setup video; the review follows.

----

Daniel picked his whole squad based on the movies the minis reference rather than their in-game stats. When you build a party, does theme and looks win out over optimisation for you too?
